name: NumPy
what_it_does: NumPy provides N-dimensional array objects and vectorized mathematical operations for efficient numerical computing in Python.
use_cases: Transform tabular data into arrays for batch numerical processing without Python loops
Perform matrix multiplication and linear algebra operations for ML feature engineering
Apply broadcasting rules to operate on arrays of different shapes without manual reshaping
Use fancy indexing and boolean masks to filter and select data subsets efficiently
Convert between dtypes and manage memory layout for interoperability with C extensions or BLAS
not_for: Symbolic math or exact rational arithmetic (use sympy instead)
Out-of-core or distributed array processing on data larger than RAM (use dask or cupy)
Labeled or heterogeneous tabular data with mixed types (use pandas instead)
best_when: You need fast, vectorized math on homogeneous numerical arrays that fit in memory.
avoid_when: Your data is heterogeneous, labeled, or larger than available RAM.

auth.notes: Pure Python library, no authentication required.

pricing.notes: Free and open source under BSD license.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Shape mismatch errors are informative but broadcasting errors can be cryptic for agents unfamiliar with NumPy broadcasting rules.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Pure functions are fully idempotent; in-place operations (e.g. +=) mutate arrays.
known_agent_gotchas: View vs copy semantics: slicing returns a view, fancy indexing returns a copy — mutating a slice mutates the original array unexpectedly
dtype precision loss: integer arrays silently truncate float results (e.g. np.array([1,2,3]) / 2 returns [0,1,1] in Python 2 style; always check dtype before arithmetic)
Broadcasting rules require trailing dimensions to match or be 1 — mismatched shapes raise ValueError but the fix is non-obvious without understanding the rule
C-contiguous vs Fortran-contiguous memory order affects performance of operations passed to external BLAS/LAPACK; np.ascontiguousarray() may be needed
np.nan comparisons always return False — use np.isnan() not == np.nan, and NaN propagates silently through aggregations unless nanmean/nansum variants are used

security_score_components.security_notes: No network surface; security concerns are limited to supply-chain hygiene and safe deserialization — avoid np.load() with allow_pickle=True on untrusted data.
